finding: On April 12 and April 14, work spanned 7-8 distinct project contexts per day (nixos-config, jhana, claude-marketplace, whisper-writer, autodoro, dotfiles, jonathans-assistant, etc.). While each project individually connects to a stated goal, the sheer breadth of daily context-switching suggests difficulty prioritizing which goals to advance deeply. The user's goal list spans 4 categories with ~15 items — trying to move many of them forward in a single day risks shallow progress everywhere rather than meaningful depth anywhere. This pattern intensified in the most recent days (Apr 12: 7 projects, Apr 14: 8 projects) compared to earlier weeks (typically 1-3 projects per day).
existing_mitigation: None found. No CLAUDE.md rule, hook, or skill addresses daily focus or project prioritization.

finding: Three sessions (March 19-30) were spent building and running ArtCraft, an open-source 'IDE for artists' project. This does not map to any stated goal in config.json — it is not one of the user's apps (Jhana, Intender), not infrastructure work (NixOS, Claude Code), not impact tech (d/acc, veganism, gov tech), and not relationship/community work. It could connect loosely to 'freedom of self-expression' or 'dynamic range' in the north star, but no connection field covers it. If this is intentional exploration or a friend's project, consider updating goals to reflect it; otherwise it may represent drift.
